title: Advancing operational global aerosol forecasting with machine learning
methods: latitude-weighted RMSE and spatial Pearson R evaluation
data_sources: AERONET ground-based sun photometer network (V3.0, L2.0)
SDS-WAS dust models: FMI-SILAM, CMA-CUACE/Dust, JMA-MASINGAR, KMA-ADAM3

data_scope.spatial_coverage: Global at ~50 km (0.5 deg x 0.625 deg); East Asia regional for dust model comparison
data_scope.temporal_coverage_operational: Operational since 20 January 2025; Jul-Aug 2024 used for vs-GEOS-FP comparison
data_scope.variables_forecast: 54 total: 12 aerosol variables (AOD, TSAOD, SUAOD, DUAOD, BCAOD, OCAOD, SSAOD, SUSMC, DUSMC, BCSMC, OCSMC, SSSMC) + 6 surface + 4 upper-air meteorological variables at 9 pressure levels
data_scope.evaluation_sites_aeronet: All available global sites for 2023 and Jul-Aug 2024 (exact count not stated in text)
data_scope.model_parameters: ~1.2 billion per base model; 4 base models in relay
data_scope.inference_speed: 5-day forecast in ~39 s on 1x L40 GPU (vs 4-6 h for GEOS-FP, approx 360x faster)

core_conclusion: AI-GAMFS, a vision transformer + U-Net model trained on 42 years of MERRA-2 reanalysis, delivers global 5-day 3-hourly aerosol forecasts in under 1 minute, outperforming CAMS and four regional dust models in AOD and DUAOD skill, and improving surface aerosol component forecasts over the USA and China compared with GEOS-FP at ~360x lower computational cost.
why_it_matters: First operational ML system for simultaneous global multi-component aerosol forecasting; 360x speed-up enabling 8-times-daily real-world updates; 3-4 day advance warning of dust-emission intensification; directly reduces mortality risk from dust storms and wildfires; fully open operational system live since Jan 2025.
why_nature: Cross-disciplinary synthesis of atmospheric chemistry, NWP and frontier deep learning; planetary-scale societal impact on air quality and health; rigorously benchmarked against CAMS and GEOS-FP (world-leading operational baselines); infrastructure-level contribution via live public forecast platform.
added_value_to_my_research: Probabilistic aerosol forecast uncertainty propagation into 2SFCA/R2SFCA health-exposure accessibility models
Learned downscaling from 50-km AI-GAMFS to 1-km neighborhood-scale PM2.5 for census-tract health analysis
Physics-informed graph neural network priors (transport conservation) transferable from aerosol to urban pollution GNNs
Dynamic population-weighted aerosol exposure surface by coupling AI-GAMFS with mobility data for time-varying health-risk accessibility
Component-resolved aerosol (BC/OC/sulfate) as SHAP-interpretable feature channels in spatially varying emission-source attribution models

related.title: Probabilistic weather forecasting with machine learning
related.relation: Probabilistic ML forecasting methodology to extend AI-GAMFS from deterministic to uncertainty-quantified outputs

related.title: First close insight into global daily gapless 1 km PM2.5 pollution, variability, and health impact
related.relation: ML-based fine-resolution aerosol/pollution mapping; natural downscaling target for AI-GAMFS 50-km outputs

related.title: Stratospheric impacts on dust transport and air pollution in West Africa and the Eastern Mediterranean
related.relation: Long-range dust transport dynamics; stratospheric physics priors for physics-informed AI-GAMFS extension

related.title: Predicting human mobility flows in cities using deep learning on satellite imagery
related.relation: Mobility flow modeling to couple with AI-GAMFS for dynamic population-weighted aerosol exposure estimation

related.title: Empirical evidence of air pollution reduction from electric vehicle usage across Chinese cities
related.relation: Urban air pollution policy attribution; explainable ML for source-type aerosol impact quantification in Chinese cities

related.title: Aerosol breezes drive cloud and precipitation increases
related.relation: Aerosol-cloud-precipitation feedback dynamics; complementary mechanistic understanding of aerosol spatial distribution effects

figures.caption: Architecture of AI-GAMFS: cube embedding, vision transformer, and cube unembedding backbone with skip connections; relay forecasting strategy integrating four pretrained base models (3-h, 6-h, 9-h, 12-h).
Superior global AOD and DUAOD forecasting throughout 2023: R and RMSE time series vs. MERRA-2 and AERONET for AI-GAMFS, operational AI-GAMFS, and CAMS; spatial RMSE distribution at AERONET sites.

Scorecard comparison of 12 aerosol variable forecasts (spatial R and latitude-weighted RMSE) between operational AI-GAMFS and GEOS-FP for Jul-Aug 2024; independent IMPROVE/AERONET evaluated surface component accuracy.

Case study: 3-day lead global forecasts for AOD and 5 optical components (SUAOD, DUAOD, BCAOD, OCAOD, SSAOD) from AI-GAMFS, GEOS-FP and MERRA-2 (initialized 22:30 UTC 26 July 2024); error maps and accuracy metrics.
